Psychotria molliramis is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Malaysia and Indonesia. The plant is classified as a member of the Rubiaceae family, which includes coffee and gardenia plants. intro: Psychotria molliramis, also known as Mollisleaf Psychotria, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria molliramis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Psychotria molliramis is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
